af_score_components.notes: MCP server with good quality (80). Read-only mode by default is excellent for safe AI use — key differentiator from db-mcp-server. Row limits truncate results silently — agents may not detect incomplete data. SSH tunnel support for secure remote access. Custom parameterized tools via TOML. Web workbench included. Zero dependencies reduces operational complexity significantly.

security_score_components.security_notes: SSL/TLS supported for database connections. SSH tunnel available for secure remote access. Read-only default mode is an excellent security guardrail. No MCP-level authentication — local access control only. Credentials via DSN string or TOML config. Bytebase-maintained with reasonable dependency hygiene. Unknown version introduces some uncertainty.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA — depends on target database availability. Unknown version. No documented breaking change policy. Read-only default provides good error recovery (queries can be safely retried). Row limit boundary creates predictable but silent truncation. SSH tunnel adds a reliability dependency for remote database access.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with good quality (80). Two separate APIs: Admin REST API (jobs, environments) and Discovery/Metadata GraphQL API (model lineage). Run triggers are async — agents must poll for completion. dbt Cloud's MCP wraps dbt Core CLI, not Cloud API directly — behavior differs between local/cloud contexts. Account ID, project ID, and environment ID all required. Webhook payloads unsigned by default.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Service tokens recommended over admin tokens — use minimum necessary permissions. No fine-grained scope on service tokens. SOC2/GDPR. Service tokens via environment variables. Webhook payloads not signed by default — verify source IP for security-sensitive workflows. dbt-labs maintained with active security practices.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Managed dbt Cloud with SLA and status page. Stable versioning. Migration guides available. Retry guidance documented. Async run polling is required — agents need to implement polling loop. Two-API architecture (Admin + Discovery) creates version coordination concern.

af_score_components.notes: Official dbt Labs MCP server (quality 82). Supports both local dbt Core and dbt Cloud contexts — feature availability varies by context. text_to_sql quality depends heavily on project documentation. MCPB distribution format requires MCPB-aware clients. Cross-project search is alpha. Admin API operations trigger real production side effects. v1.9.2 — reasonably mature for an MCP tool.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for Cloud API calls. API keys + local project config — mix of credential types. No scope granularity on API tokens. Official dbt Labs maintained — v1.9.2 with active development. API keys via environment variables. Admin API operations (job triggers) have real production side effects — use with caution.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Hybrid reliability: local dbt Core is self-managed; Cloud features depend on dbt Cloud uptime. v1.9.2 reasonably stable. dbt-labs maintains breaking changes across versions. No retry guidance. Partial idempotency. Feature availability depends on dbt Core vs Cloud vs Fusion mode — agents must know their context.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with moderate quality (65). No API keys or auth needed — zero cost for search. Scraping-based access to Bing, Brave, DuckDuckGo, Google, Yahoo, etc. May break without notice when upstream engines change. Rate limits opaque and upstream-engine-dependent. MCP server requires running the API server separately. Book search via Anna's Archive has legal concerns in some jurisdictions.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS used for all upstream search engine connections. No credentials needed or stored — minimal security surface. Scraping-based approach creates legal and technical risk from upstream terms of service. Unknown version and dependency audit status. Proxy support helps avoid rate limiting but introduces proxy trust considerations.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted, entirely dependent on upstream search engine availability. Unknown version — scraping layer may break without notice. No documented breaking change policy. Full GET idempotency on queries. Results quality and availability varies by backend engine. Not suitable for production workflows requiring consistent search results.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with limited quality (55). Reports take ~2 minutes — agents must handle long-running operations. Requires configuring both thinking and task LLM models. MCP server requires full web app to be running. Supports multiple LLM + search provider combinations. Good for privacy-focused self-hosted research. Not a standalone MCP binary.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on deployment configuration (reverse proxy recommended). Access password for web UI, bearer token for API. Multiple LLM and search provider API keys required in environment variables. No scope granularity. Unknown version and dependency audit status. Self-hosted means operator is responsible for security hardening.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. Unknown version — unstable API surface. 2-minute report generation creates timeout and error handling challenges. Dependent on both LLM and search providers being available simultaneously. Partial idempotency. Multiple integration points create high failure probability for production use.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Excellent documentation and multi-language SDK coverage. Real-time streaming uses WebSocket (wss://) — different protocol from REST; agents need WS client capability. Audio format must be specified correctly — wrong mimetype causes silent failures. Callback URLs for async transcription must be publicly accessible. Smart formatting injects punctuation into transcription text.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS and WSS enforced. API key with role-based scopes (Member, Admin, Owner) plus per-capability scoping. Project-scoped keys available. SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA — strong compliance for audio/voice data. API key via environment variable. Callback URLs for async transcription must use HTTPS and validate signatures.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Managed SaaS with SLA and status page. Stable API versioning with multi-language SDK parity. Migration guides available. Retry guidance documented. No idempotency on transcription — retrying creates duplicate charges. Async transcription via callback requires publicly accessible endpoints for production reliability.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with good quality (78). Excellent documentation with multi-language SDK. Free API keys end in ':fx' — using one for Pro features returns 403. HTML tag handling requires explicit parameter or markup gets mangled. Glossaries require pre-creation via separate API call. Language codes use regional variants requiring explicit specification. Full idempotency on translation calls.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key via DeepL-Auth-Key header — no scope granularity. GDPR/ISO27001 compliance — European company with strong data protection. Free/Pro key distinction via ':fx' suffix is a design choice but not a security boundary. API key via environment variable. Established translation platform with good security practices.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Managed SaaS with SLA and status page. Stable versioning. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Full idempotency on translation (same text + same params = same result). Rate limits vary by tier — document clearly for agent workflows. Character counting includes spaces and tags — agents should pre-calculate costs.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. OpenAPI spec available. Clean, developer-friendly API. Rate limit of 250/5 min can be hit quickly when provisioning resources. Droplet creation is async — agents must poll action status. No native idempotency keys — check before creating. SSH keys must be pre-added before Droplet creation. Floating IP and Reserved IP are different resources. Snapshot operations take many minutes.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Personal access tokens (full or read-only) or OAuth2. Read-only PATs are excellent for monitoring agents. OAuth2 scopes for third-party integrations. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR. PAT via environment variable. SSH key management integrated into account. Good security model for cloud infrastructure.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Cloud SaaS with SLA and status page. OpenAPI-backed stable API. Migration guides available. Retry guidance documented. Async Droplet creation requires polling — must implement with appropriate timeout. No idempotency keys means duplicate creation risk on retry — always check existence first. Good overall reliability posture.

af_score_components.notes: MCP orchestration platform (quality 75). Tool filtering via playbooks preserves context window space — core value proposition. Manages OAuth centrally for downstream MCP servers. AGPL v3 license has copyleft implications for commercial use. Requires Bun as package manager. Turborepo monorepo build complexity for contributors. v1.1.1 — moderate maturity for an orchestration layer.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local operation with no MCP-level auth needed. HTTPS for OAuth flows to downstream services. Centralized OAuth management is a good security pattern — reduces per-agent credential sprawl. AGPL v3 creates open-source obligations. Bun runtime dependency. Playbook-based tool filtering provides logical scope control but not hard security boundaries. v1.1.1 with moderate maturity.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. v1.1.1 — relatively stable for core features. No documented breaking change policy. Unknown idempotency behavior. Orchestration layer adds a new single point of failure for all downstream MCP connections. CLI with 30+ commands provides operational flexibility.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Excellent multi-language SDK coverage. Rate limits are per-route, not global — agents must track per-endpoint limits. Gateway (WebSocket) required for real-time events — REST-only bots cannot receive messages. Message content intent requires approval for bots in 100+ servers. Slash command registration is separate and requires guild/global scope decisions. 3-second deadline on interaction responses.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS and WSS enforced. Bot tokens + OAuth2 with Gateway intents provide granular permission control. OAuth2 scopes limit what bots can access per server. COPPA/GDPR compliance for community platform. Bot tokens via environment variables. Webhook URLs are auth-independent — validate webhook signatures. Discord maintains good security practices for a consumer platform.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Consumer SaaS with status page and reasonable SLA. Stable versioned API with migration guides. Good backward compatibility history. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. No message delivery idempotency — retrying sends may duplicate messages. Per-route rate limits require sophisticated retry logic. Gateway reconnection handling is important for long-running bots.
